content.title: Mechanism-Guided Selective Unlearning for RLVR-Induced Reasoning
content.summary: This paper proposes a mechanism-guided selective unlearning approach for removing undesired behaviors from LLMs trained with RLVR, allowing fine-grained control over which reasoning patterns are erased without degrading overall model performance across 15 pages with 4 figures and 7 tables of empirical evidence.
content.summary_1: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) produces powerful reasoning capabilities in LLMs, but can also induce undesired behaviors or biases that are difficult to remove post-training without destroying useful capabilities. This work introduces Mechanism-Guided Selective Unlearning, which identifies the internal mechanisms through which RLVR-induced reasoning patterns are encoded and uses those mechanistic insights to perform targeted unlearning—erasing specific undesired behaviors while preserving beneficial reasoning skills. The approach provides a principled framework for AI safety practitioners to correct undesired post-training artifacts in deployed reasoning models. With 15 pages, 4 figures, and 7 tables of results, the paper provides comprehensive empirical validation of selective unlearning effectiveness across different model architectures and reasoning task types.
content.body_text: Mechanism-Guided Selective Unlearning for RLVR-Induced Reasoning by Chenyu Zhou, Qiliang Jiang, Shuning Wu, and Xu Zhou addresses the challenge of removing specific undesired behaviors from LLMs that have been post-trained using Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). RLVR training can induce strong reasoning capabilities, but may also introduce problematic patterns. The authors propose to first understand the mechanisms by which RLVR embeds these patterns in model weights, then use that mechanistic understanding to guide selective unlearning that removes only the targeted behaviors. The work includes 15 pages of analysis with 4 figures and 7 tables. Subjects: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI).

tech_insights.impact_summary: Mechanism-guided selective unlearning is a critical capability for safely deploying RLVR-trained models in production—allowing post-deployment correction of undesired behaviors without full retraining. This directly enables safer iterative improvement of reasoning AI systems.
actions_developer: Explore mechanism-guided unlearning to remove specific failure modes from RLVR-trained reasoning models without full retraining
Apply selective unlearning framework to audit and correct safety-relevant behaviors in deployed reasoning LLMs
actions_business: Factor selective unlearning capability into AI model lifecycle management policies for post-deployment correction
Evaluate mechanism-guided unlearning as a compliance tool for removing biased or restricted outputs from deployed reasoning models
